mission.title: Install and Configure Dify
mission.description: Dify is a production-ready platform for agentic workflow development, allowing users to build and deploy AI applications efficiently. It is suitable for teams looking to leverage AI capabilities in their workflows. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and resource management.

steps.title: Add the Dify Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Dify Helm repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install Dify using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add dify https://charts.langgenius.com

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Dify Helm chart repository.
> Updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Dify CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Dify to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/langgenius/dify/main/deploy/crds.yaml

```

> This command applies the CRDs necessary for Dify.

steps.title: Install Dify using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs the Dify application in the specified namespace using Helm.

```bash

helm install dify dify/dify --namespace dify --create-namespace --version 0.12.0

```

> This command installs Dify in the 'dify' namespace.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource (CR) for Dify to demonstrate its functionality.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dify.langgenius.com/v1
kind: DifyApp
metadata:
  name: example-dify-app
spec:
  description: Example Dify application
EOF

```

> This command creates an example Dify application.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dify.langgenius.com/v1
kind: DifyApp
metadata:
  name: example-dify-app
spec:
  description: Example Dify application
EOF

steps.title: Verify Dify is running
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Dify application to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n dify

```

> This command lists the pods in the 'dify' namespace to verify they are running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Dify
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Dify application and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all data associated with Dify.

```bash

helm uninstall dify --namespace dify

kubectl delete namespace dify

```

> This command uninstalls the Dify application.
> This command deletes the 'dify' namespace and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Dify
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your data. This step upgrades Dify to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n dify -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.status.phase == "Running") | .metadata.name' | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade dify dify/dify --namespace dify --version 0.12.0

kubectl get pods -n dify

```

> Drain the running pods before upgrading.
> This command upgrades Dify to the specified version.
> Verify that the Dify pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n dify -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.status.phase == "Running") | .metadata.name' | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "DifyApp" in version "dify.langgenius.com/v1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the CRD for DifyApp has not been installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRDs using 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/langgenius/dify/main/deploy/crds.yaml'.

troubleshooting.title: Pod is in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates an issue with the application configuration or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n dify' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Kubernetes API server is not reachable or the cluster is under heavy load.

**Fix:**
Check the cluster status and ensure that the API server is healthy.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that your kubeconfig is not set up correctly or the cluster is not running.

**Fix:**
Ensure your kubeconfig is pointing to the correct cluster and that the cluster is up.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new AI workflows and improved integration with existing tools.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated old API endpoints for workflow management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 0.11, update your CRDs and review the new API documentation.

resolution.summary: You should see the Dify application running with the command 'kubectl get pods -n dify' showing all pods in 'Running' state.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dify.langgenius.com/v1
kind: DifyApp
metadata:
  name: example-dify-app
spec:
  description: Example Dify application
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the specified resources.
